mission.title: Install and Configure Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK)
mission.description: Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK) automates the deployment and management of Elastic Stack components on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking to run Elasticsearch, Kibana, and other Elastic services in a Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the complexity of managing Kubernetes resources and the need for familiarity with the Elastic Stack.

steps.title: Add the Elastic Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Elastic Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install ECK.

```bash

helm repo add elastic https://helm.elastic.co

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Elastic Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are required for ECK to manage Elastic Stack resources. This step installs them.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/eck/2.14.0/crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for ECK.

steps.title: Install ECK Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the ECK operator in the 'elastic-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --create-namespace --version 2.14.0

```

> Installs the ECK operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create an ElasticSearch Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates an Elasticsearch cluster using a custom resource definition.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.6.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF

```

> Creates a 3-node Elasticsearch cluster.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.6.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Elasticsearch cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n default

kubectl get pods -n default

```

> Verify that the Elasticsearch cluster is running.
> Check that all pods are in the Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ECK Operator
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the ECK operator and all associated resources. Data loss may occur if the Elasticsearch cluster is not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall eck-operator --namespace elastic-system

kubectl delete elasticsearch my-elasticsearch -n default

```

> Uninstalls the ECK operator.
> Deletes the Elasticsearch cluster, which may result in data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ECK Operator
upgrade.description:
Backup your Elasticsearch data, cordon nodes if necessary, upgrade the operator, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch my-elasticsearch -o yaml > my-elasticsearch-backup.yaml

helm upgrade eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.1

kubectl get elasticsearch -n default

```

> Backup the current Elasticsearch configuration.
> Upgrades the ECK operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the Elasticsearch cluster is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get elasticsearch my-elasticsearch -o yaml > my-elasticsearch-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Elasticsearch cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Elasticsearch cluster may not have enough resources or may be misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation and configuration settings in the Elasticsearch CRD.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Elasticsearch pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient permissions for the service account used by the ECK operator.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service account has the necessary permissions to create pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The required Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) may not be installed.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/eck/2.14.0/crds.yaml.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Elasticsearch pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with kubectl logs <pod-name> and adjust the configuration or resource limits accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Elasticsearch 8.6, improved TLS management, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Elasticsearch versions below 8.0.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.12 or 2.13, ensure to update your Elasticsearch CRDs and configurations to match the new version requirements.

resolution.summary: You should see the Elasticsearch cluster status as 'Green' when running 'kubectl get elasticsearch -n default'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.6.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the required tools installed.
